'''David Faiumu''' (born 30 April 1983) is a former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 2000s and 2010s. He is currently an assistant coach for the [[North Queensland Cowboys Women]] in the [[NRL Women's Premiership]] (NRLW) and the [[New Zealand women's national rugby league team]].

A New Zealand and Samoan international representative, he played for the [[North Queensland Cowboys]] in the [[National Rugby League]] (NRL) and [[Huddersfield Giants]] in the [[Super League]].<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

==Background==
Born in [[Wellington, New Zealand]], Faiumu is of [[Samoans|Samoan]] descent and played his junior rugby league for the [[Randwick Kingfishers]] and [[Wainuiomata Lions]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.nzherald.co.nz/sport/rugby-league/league-homecoming-a-special-one-for-kiwis-hooker/I4CAFGJAMAT2TJ4W2WHFLNBSEU/|title=Homecoming a special one for Kiwis hooker|date=10 October 2007|website=[[The New Zealand Herald]] }}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://websites.mygameday.app/get_file.cgi?id=35950792|title=The Randwick Rave|date=1 July 2016|website=My Gameday}}</ref>

==Playing career==
In 2000, Faiumu played for Wainuiomata as a 17-year-old in the inaugural season of the [[Bartercard Cup]] before being signed by the [[Canberra Raiders]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.nzherald.co.nz/sport/rugby-league/rugby-league-plenty-of-drama-in-bruising-club-league-semifinals/MUDBRVUDQBJDKPSNIZQNE3J6UA/|title=Plenty of drama in bruising club league semifinals|date=27 August 2000|website=[[The New Zealand Herald]] }}</ref>

He spent two seasons playing for the Raiders' [[Jersey Flegg Cup]] side and represented the Junior Kiwis in 2001.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.nzherald.co.nz/sport/rugby-league/nrl/league-kiwi-turns-out-to-be-a-good-fit-at-townsville/CVJLZE7ZQINH7PEV3REKK4XCQQ/|title=Kiwi turns out to be a good fit at Townsville|date=1 July 2005|website=[[The New Zealand Herald]] }}</ref>

In 2003, Faiumu left Canberra, joining the [[Central Comets]] in the [[Queensland Cup]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/faiumu-commits-to-cowboys/news-story/348c0b893b6486c19d34ec050acf80b0|title=Faiumu commits to Cowboys|date=17 March 2009|website=Fox Sports Australia}}</ref> After one season at the club, he was signed by the [[North Queensland Cowboys]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://leagueunlimited.com/news/gamble-named-comets-best-for-2003/|title=Gamble Named Comets Best For 2003|date=10 October 2003|website=League Unlimited}}</ref>

===North Queensland Cowboys===
In Round 11 of the [[2004 NRL season]], Faiumu made his NRL debut, coming off the bench in a 22–18 loss to the [[Penrith Panthers]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/seasons/nrl-2004/round-11/north-qld-vs-penrith/summary.html|title=North Queensland Cowboys 18 lost to Penrith Panthers 22|date=|website=Rugby League Project}}</ref>

In 2005, he became a regular in the Cowboys' 17, playing 21 games, which included coming off the bench in the club's first ever [[2005 NRL Grand Final|Grand Final]], which they lost to the [[Wests Tigers]]. In October 2005, he was selected to play for the [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] in the [[2005 Rugby League Tri-Nations#Final|Tri-Nations]], coming off the bench in their 24–0 final victory over [[Australia national rugby league team|Australia]].<ref>{{cite web|title=Anzac Test Match2007|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/matches/anzac-test-match-2007/australia-vs-new-zealand.html|work=rugbyleagueproject.org|publisher=Shawn Dollin, Andrew Ferguson and Bill Bates|access-date=1 September 2013}}</ref>

===Huddersfield Giants===
On 7 June 2008, after four-and-a-half seasons with the Cowboys, Faiumu was granted an early release from his contract to join the [[Huddersfield Giants]].<ref>[http://www.townsvillebulletin.com.au/article/2008/06/07/13735_cowboys.html Article] from ''[[The Townsville Bulletin]]''</ref>

In 2009, he started at lock in the [[2009 Challenge Cup final|Challenge Cup]] final, which Huddersfield lost 25–16 to the [[Warrington Wolves]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2009/aug/29/challenge-cup-warrington-huddersfield|title=Warrington take the biggest prize with first Wembley win in 35 years|date=30 August 2009|website=[[The Guardian]] }}</ref> On 16 October 2010, Faiumu represented [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]] in their 50–6 loss to New Zealand.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2010/10/08/toa-samoa-names-team-to-face-nz/|title=Toa Samoa names team to face NZ|date=8 October 2010|website=NRL}}</ref>

In 2011 and 2012, Faiumu represented the [[Exiles rugby league team|Exiles]] against [[England national rugby league team|England]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/13278410|title=Brian McClennan completes Exiles squad to face England fixture|work=BBC Sport|date=4 May 2011|archive-date=13 January 2016|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160113141331/https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/13278410|url-status=live}}</ref>

At the end of the 2014 season, following seven years at the club, Faiumu announced his retirement and took up a position as the Giants' Developmental Officer.<ref>{{cite web|title=David Faiumu leaves Huddersfield Giants after play-off loss|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/29394155|website=BBC Sport|access-date=18 January 2015|date=27 September 2014}}</ref>

==Coaching career==
In October 2017, Faiumu returned to [[Rockhampton, Queensland]], taking up a role as assistant coach for the [[Central Queensland Capras]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.couriermail.com.au/news/queensland/rockhampton/sport/faiumu-taps-into-cqs-raw-talent/news-story/6c1746c2af0be81c7b6aabbd0e37c800|title=Faiumu taps into CQ's 'raw talent'|date=6 December 2017|website=The Courier Mail}}</ref>

In 2019, Faiumu replaced Kim Williams as head coach of the Capras, a position he held until January 2021.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.qrl.com.au/news/20182/08/15/capras-new-coach-is-ex-kiwi-test-rep/|title=Capras' new coach is ex Kiwi Test rep|date=15 August 2018|website=QRL}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.qrl.com.au/news/2021/01/11/faiumu-steps-down-as-capras-head-coach/|title=Faiumu steps down as Capras head coach|date=11 January 2021|website=QRL}}</ref>

In 2022, he became assistant coach of the [[New Zealand women's national rugby league team|Kiwi Ferns]] under head coach [[Ricky Henry (rugby league)|Ricky Henry]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.iheart.com/podcast/1049-the-all-sport-breakfast-72836273/episode/david-faiumu-kiwis-ferns-v-jillaroos-104836863/|title=David Faiumu: Kiwis Ferns v Jillaroos RLWC Final|date=19 November 2022|website=iHeart}}</ref>

On 18 April 2024, Faiumu returned to the Cowboys as an assistant coach to their [[North Queensland Cowboys Women|women's]] side.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.cowboys.com.au/news/2024/04/18/faiumu-back-in-cowboys-colours1/|title=Faiumu back in Cowboys colours|date=18 April 2024|website=NQ Cowboys}}</ref>
